text: This is a twenty-two year old lady who presented with pain during menses. Ultrasound showed a small right ovarian cyst.
The surgeon performs single incision laparoscopy using a three-part technique, with visible confirmation that both fallopian tubes are patent.
The surgeon opens the endometriotic cyst and releases it from the lateral pelvic wall. Vasopressin is injected into the surgical site, followed by the cystectomy procedure to remove the cyst.
The surgeon coagulates visible endometriotic spots, followed by placing the excised cyst wall into a surgical retrieval bag.
The surgeon removes the bag containing the cyst from the umbilical wound and proceeds to suture the wound, completing the closure process.
